title: bupleurum angulosum var. alpinum
intro: Bupleurum angulosum var. alpinum, also known as the alpine knotted hedge parsley or alpine hare's ear, is a plant species that belongs to the genus Bupleurum, a group of flowering plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Bupleurum angulosum var. alpinum have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of cold and flu, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
